<!-- _class: center -->

# Strengthening KBC Financial Operations

<p class="subtitle">A plan for clarity, accountability, and sustainability</p>

<div class="callout">
<p>Moving carefully, communicating plainly, and strengthening the system together.</p>
</div>

<!--
Speaker notes:

Tonight is about helping the church understand the direction of the financial operations work. We are trying to be clear without overwhelming everyone with details. We are also grateful for the people who have served and for those helping during the transition.
-->

---

## Why We Are Talking About This

- KBC is in a Treasurer transition.
- Interim coverage is helping maintain continuity.
- This gives us an opportunity to strengthen the system.

<div class="callout">
<p>This is a stewardship moment, not a blame moment.</p>
</div>

<!--
Speaker notes:

We want to start with gratitude. People have carried important financial work for the church, and we are thankful for that service. The transition gives us a chance to make the system clearer and easier to hand off in the future.
-->

---

## What This Is / What This Is Not

<div class="grid two">
<div class="card">
<h3>This is</h3>
<p>A careful review process to clarify responsibilities and strengthen accountability.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>This is not</h3>
<p>A blame discussion or a final vote on every detail tonight.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>This is</h3>
<p>A way to put the right questions in the right committee lanes.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>This is not</h3>
<p>A software decision tonight.</p>
</div>
</div>

<!--
Speaker notes:

This slide is important because it lowers the temperature. We are not here to assign blame, and we are not asking the church to approve every detail tonight. We are asking for support for a careful process.
-->

---

## What We Have Learned

- Too much depends on individual knowledge.
- The current role is hard to hand off.
- Financial work has become more complex.
- Software helps, but structure comes first.

<!--
Speaker notes:

The lesson is not that anyone did something wrong. The lesson is that the work has grown and the system needs to be clearer. Software can help later, but software will not fix unclear responsibilities by itself.
-->

---

## Proposed Direction

- Treasurer remains an accountable elected role.
- Paid part-time bookkeeping support has been authorized.
- Finance owns financial process.
- Personnel owns job description and hiring.
- Church votes where required.

<!--
Speaker notes:

The main idea is simple: keep accountability clear, support the routine work appropriately, and let each committee do the part that belongs to it. The church has already authorized paid part-time bookkeeping support, and any other decision that requires church approval will still come back to the church.
-->

---

## Simple Structure Visual

| Area | Primary Focus |
| --- | --- |
| Treasurer / Financial Officer | Accountability and reporting |
| Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator | Day-to-day records |
| Finance Committee | Policy, software, budget, oversight |
| Personnel Committee | Job description, application, hiring |
| Nominating Committee | Coordination named in the motion |
| Congregation | Approval where required |

<!--
Speaker notes:

This is the simplest way to picture the proposed structure. The Treasurer remains accountable. The authorized Bookkeeper or Financial Administrator role would help with routine records. Finance and Personnel each have their proper lanes, and the congregation votes where required.
-->

---

## Divide and Conquer

<div class="grid three">
<div class="card">
<h3>Interim process</h3>
<p>Document must-do financial tasks.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>Software</h3>
<p>Evaluate tools after requirements are clear.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>Policies</h3>
<p>Review reimbursement and spending practices.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>Roles</h3>
<p>Finalize job description, application, and hiring process.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>Monthly review</h3>
<p>Build a steady Finance Committee rhythm.</p>
</div>
<div class="card">
<h3>Church approval</h3>
<p>Confirm what must come back for a vote.</p>
</div>
</div>

<!--
Speaker notes:

This work does not have to be solved all at once. We can divide it into practical workstreams. That helps us move forward carefully and keeps committees from getting overloaded or crossing into each other's responsibilities.
-->

---

## Next 30 Days

- Confirm interim coverage.
- Document must-do financial tasks.
- Finalize Bookkeeper job description and application.
- Finance confirms duties and controls.
- Personnel begins the hiring process.

<!--
Speaker notes:

The next month is about stabilizing and moving from authorization to implementation. Finance should confirm financial duties and controls. Personnel should finalize the job description, application, and hiring process. Leadership should keep track of what may still need bylaw review or a church vote.
-->

---

## What We Are Asking From the Church

<div class="grid two">
<div class="card"><h3>Prayer</h3><p>For wisdom and unity.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3>Patience</h3><p>For a careful process.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3>Support</h3><p>For the authorized hiring process.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3>Service</h3><p>For willingness to help where needed.</p></div>
</div>

<!--
Speaker notes:

We are asking the church to support the process. That means praying, being patient, encouraging unity, and letting Personnel and Finance do their work in the lanes the church has set. There may also be places where people can serve as needs become clearer.
-->

---

<!-- _class: center -->

## Closing

<p class="scripture">This is about faithful stewardship and long-term health.</p>

<p class="scripture">"Let all things be done decently and in order." &mdash; 1 Corinthians 14:40</p>

<!--
Speaker notes:

The hope is that this strengthens KBC for the long run. We want to honor those who have served, support those who are serving now, and make the work clearer for whoever serves next. We will bring future votes back to the church where they are required.
-->
